title: Misty Smoky Tunisian Couscous Tagine
description: A brunch‑mood, smoky pillowy couscous tagine with red pepper paste, kombu cold extract, and shojin‑friendly flavor.
instructions: In a bowl, combine couscous, 150 ml water, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt, and 0.5 tsp black pepper, cover loosely, and let steam for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium heat, add diced onion and minced garlic, and sauté until fragrant.
Add sliced red pepper to the skillet, cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in tomato paste and smoked paprika, cooking for 2 minutes to bloom flavors.
Pour in 200 ml water and the kombu cold extract, bring to a simmer, and let thicken into a sauce for about 5 minutes.
Gently fold the cooked couscous into the sauce, ensuring even coating.
Transfer the mixture to a shallow oven‑proof dish, cover with foil, and bake for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove from oven, fluff the couscous, and stir in chopped cilantro.
Serve warm with a side of lemon wedges if desired.
